variant: give me the complete guide: table of products, weekly timeline, checklist, and key stats for starting skincare from zero

request.message: give me the complete guide: table of products, weekly timeline, checklist, and key stats for starting skincare from zero

response_body.response:
I'm building this from the barrier-first principles in your docs, plus general skincare foundations. your evidence is thin on specific product names and dosages, so I'm filling the structure with industry-standard starter picks. --- **start here: barrier first, one active later.** the biggest mistake is product overload.

you need a cleanser, moisturizer with ceramides + panthenol, and SPF. that's it for week 1.

once your barrier is solid (no redness, stinging, or tightness), add one active—either a gentle retinoid or azelaic acid depending on your main concern. your skin type matters.

if you're oily/acne-prone, you'll cleanse daily and eventually add azelaic AM + retinoid PM. if dry/sensitive, cleanse once daily and prioritize hydration + barrier repair first. --- **product table (starter stack):**  --- **weekly timeline (weeks 1–4):**  --- **checklist (your action items):**  --- **key stats:**  --- **why this order matters:** a damaged barrier makes every product useless and every active worse. ceramides and panthenol repair it; SPF prevents UV from undoing your work.

once your skin stops reacting to basic products, you're ready for an active. don't rush it.

products.name: La Roche-Posay Anthelios Melt-in Milk SPF 50+
products.description: High-protection broad-spectrum SPF 50 with cosmetic-elegant feel.

products.name: CeraVe Hydrating Facial Cleanser
products.description: Ceramide-based, fragrance-free, non-stripping — pairs with any active.

products.name: CeraVe Moisturizing Cream (Tub)
products.description: Heavy occlusive cream — good for very dry / eczema-prone skin.

products.name: The Ordinary Azelaic Acid Suspension 10%
products.description: Azelaic acid for redness, PIH, and mild rosacea — pregnancy-safe.

products.name: CeraVe PM Facial Moisturizing Lotion
products.description: Lightweight ceramide + niacinamide moisturizer for nightly use.

rows: ceramides + panthenol (CeraVe moisturizing cream or cetaphil rich)
broad-spectrum, 30+ (EltaMD UV clear or La Roche-Posay anthelios)
PM only, after barrier is solid

steps.label: week 1
steps.detail: cleanse + moisturize + SPF only.  no actives. let barrier stabilize.  watch for stinging, redness, or tightness.

steps.label: week 2
steps.detail: same routine. skin should feel less tight, less reactive.  barrier is healing.

steps.label: week 3
steps.detail: if no irritation, introduce ONE active: retinoid (low dose, 2x/week) or azelaic acid (daily). start slow.

steps.label: week 4
steps.detail: assess: is skin clearer, less inflamed?  increase active frequency only if no new irritation. stick with one active.

items: pick a gentle cleanser (non-foaming, no sulfates)
get a moisturizer with ceramides and panthenol
cleanse AM + PM, moisturize immediately after, apply SPF in AM
skip all exfoliants, acids, and retinoids for week 1
after week 2, if skin feels stable, add ONE active (retinoid or azelaic)
do not stack actives.  one at a time.
reapply SPF every 2 hours if outdoors (or once daily minimum)

cards.label: active per routine
cards.hint: no stacking acids, retinoids, vitamin c together

cards.label: SPF non-negotiable
cards.hint: UV worsens pigmentation, breaks collagen, increases inflammation

cards.label: realistic timeline to first visible change
